title: cyathea hirsuta
intro: Cyathea hirsuta, also known as the hairy tree fern, is a species of tree fern native to New Zealand. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, and is known for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is said to offer various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, Cyathea hirsuta is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to leverage its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
